title: NOC 14111 – Data Entry Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Data Entry Clerks play a vital role in Canada’s information-driven economy by maintaining accurate digital records, processing business documents, and supporting administrative operations across public and private sectors in Canada. Classified under NOC 14111, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—offering practical immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growth of digital recordkeepingExpansion of remote and centralized operationsEntry-level TEER occupation with career mobilityEmployer-supported recruitmentData compliance and reporting requirements
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1411 – Survey, statistical, and data entry occupations

duties: Data Entry Clerks may perform some or all of the following:
Receive and register invoices, forms, records, and other documents for data capture
Input data into computerized databases, spreadsheets, or templates using keyboards, scanners, voice-recognition software, or other tools
Import and export data between different software systems
Identify, label, and organize electronic storage media
Maintain libraries of electronic storage systems
employment_requirements: College courses or certification in data entry or office systems (sometimes required)
Basic computer proficiency and keyboarding skills
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 10,300 new positions for Data Entry Clerks, with a similar number of job seekers entering the labour market—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially important for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Data Entry Clerks are in demand across both public and private sectors nationwide. Hiring has expanded rapidly since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Government departments and agenciesFinancial institutions and insurance firmsHealthcare and education organizationsLogistics and manufacturing companiesCorporate administrative offices
Annual salaries typically range between $27,000 and $63,500 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
